description: This scatter plot illustrates the relationship between specific strength (MPa·cm³/g) and tensile elongation (%) for various steel categories. The x-axis represents elongation ranging from 0 to 90%, while the y-axis displays specific strength from 0 to 350 MPa·cm³/g. Data points are categorized by steel type according to the legend: Present data (filled circles), Austenitic steels (diamonds), Ferritic steels (crosses), Martensitic steels (plus signs), Precipitation hardened steels (stars), Dual-phase steels (triangles), Super toughness steels (diamonds with crosses), TRIP steels (squares), and TWIP steels (open circles). A dashed curve indicates the approximate upper limit of present steels, showing the typical strength-ductility trade-off. The Fe-Mn-Al-C based alloys (present data) demonstrate superior specific strength compared to conventional steels, with specific compositions such as Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.8C-5Cr achieving over 180 MPa·cm³/g with approximately 40% elongation. The figure visually confirms that these low-density steels outperform traditional austenitic, ferritic, martensitic, and other advanced high-strength steels in specific strength while maintaining comparable or superior ductility.
